analysis_result: M2377 logCPM = 5.209; mean logCPM of 11 other MPNST samples = -0.474; log2 fold-change = 5.683. Fold change 2^(M2377 - mean_others) = 51.4x; CPM-scale ratio (M2377_CPM / mean_other_CPM) = 24.5x; median-based ratio = 34.2x.
discrepancy_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED: GSE207400_Matrix.txt.gz (14,995 genes x 49 samples, log2 CPM values); MOESM4 clinical data (108 samples with Methylation_Group annotations).

B. COHORT: 12 MPNST samples (Methylation_Group MPNST_G1 or MPNST_G2) present in GSE207400 matrix (M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048, M803, M1677, M2325, M2377, M2491, M4719, M9413). M1933, M3398, M6712, M9450 were MPNST samples in the clinical annotation but absent from the RNA-seq matrix (only 49 samples passed RNA-seq QC). M2377 is the target sample; 11 are 'others'.

C. VARIABLE CODING: RSPO2 log2 CPM values extracted from GSE207400_Matrix.txt.gz. M2377 logCPM = 5.209. Other 11 MPNSTs logCPM mean = -0.474, median = 0.113.

D. COMPUTATION: Three interpretations of 'fold change':
  (1) log2-space: 2^(M2377_logCPM - mean(others_logCPM)) = 2^5.683 = 51.4x
  (2) CPM-space arithmetic mean ratio: 2^M2377_logCPM / mean(2^others_logCPM) = 37.0 / 1.51 = 24.5x
  (3) Median-based ratio: 2^M2377 / 2^median(others) = 36.98 / 1.082 = 34.2x

E. RESULT: Paper reports 39.7-fold for M2377. Model's three candidates (51.4x, 24.5x, 34.2x) all fall outside +/-10% of 39.7.

F. ASSESSMENT: Discrepancy magnitudes: log2-FC interpretation +29.5%, CPM-ratio interpretation -38.3%, median-ratio interpretation -13.9%. The median-based ratio (34.2x) is the closest candidate but still outside tolerance. The paper's 39.7x is in the middle of these interpretations, suggesting the paper may have used a slightly different normalization (e.g., edgeR TMM-normalized CPM, voom-transformed values, or a different denominator set). Importantly, M1933 (reported by paper at 49.5x) is not in the RNA-seq matrix at all - this suggests the paper may have used a pre-QC or alternate expression dataset that includes more MPNST samples than the public GSE207400 matrix of 49 post-QC samples.

G. ROOT CAUSE: STATISTICAL_METHOD difference and/or missing data. Likely causes: (a) paper used TMM/normalized counts from edgeR rather than the posted log2 CPM matrix; (b) paper may have included M1933 in the calculation via a larger pre-QC matrix; (c) formula ambiguity: 'fold change' could mean log2FC-exponentiated, CPM ratio, or median-based ratio, each yielding different values.
proposed_fix: Reproduce using edgeR TMM-normalized CPM or voom-transformed counts rather than the posted log2 CPM values; alternatively, obtain the pre-QC RNA-seq matrix that includes M1933 to match the paper's denominator. If the paper computed fold change as 2^(logFC) from a moderated DE model (limma voom), this would yield a slightly different value than the simple mean-difference computation on the posted matrix.
